name: Amscot - the Money Superstore
description_short: Florida-based lender offering payday loans (cash advances) up to $500 and installment loans up to $1,000 with same-day cash disbursement, no credit checks, and 365-day availability.
description_long:
Amscot Financial has operated in Florida since 1989 as "The Money Superstore," establishing itself as a licensed and regulated short-term lending provider. The company operates exclusively within Florida, which it emphasizes has some of the strictest consumer-protection laws in the United States. Amscot is regulated by the Office of Financial Regulation (OFR) and adheres to state and federal payday loan laws, and maintains membership in INFiN with adherence to industry best practices.

Amscot's core offerings include Amscot Cash Advances (payday loans) up to $500 with no credit checks and quick cash disbursement, and Amscot Installment Cash Advances (installment loans) ranging from $100 to $1,000 allowing repayment over time, also with no credit checks. The company markets these products as more convenient than competitors because customers receive cash in hand immediately rather than checks to cash elsewhere. Beyond lending, Amscot offers ancillary financial services including check cashing, free money orders, bill payment, wire transfers, faxing, notarization, stamps, and ATM access.

Amscot distinguishes itself through its 365-day operating schedule with many 24-hour locations, immediate cash-in-hand disbursement model, and bundled financial services approach. The company explicitly markets that borrowing often costs less than overdraft fees or late payment penalties on bills, positioning short-term loans as a comparative financial tool. The website features a straightforward application process and emphasizes accessibility for consumers with bad credit or no credit history.

Amscot's payday loans carry extremely high APRs—the website discloses a 338.93% APR for a $100 advance with a 14-day term ($13 fee plus $3 verification fee). While the company operates legally and transparently, the cost structure is inherent to the payday lending model. Borrowers should carefully consider whether the convenience and speed justify the significant expense, and whether alternative solutions exist for their financial emergency.
address: 11500 S Orange Blossom Trl #3, Orlando, FL 32837

company_info.headquarters: 11500 S Orange Blossom Trl #3, Orlando, FL 32837

pros: Same-day or next-day cash in hand immediately upon approval—no waiting for checks to clear
No credit checks required; accessible to consumers with poor or no credit history
Open 365 days per year including evenings and weekends; many locations operate 24 hours
Licensed and regulated by Florida OFR; operates under state and federal payday loan laws
Simple application process explicitly described as hassle-free on their website
Installment loans up to $1,000 offer flexible repayment terms versus single-payment payday structure
Additional services bundled at one location: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, notarization, ATM
cons: Extremely high APRs (338.93% disclosed for 14-day $100 loan) create expensive debt cycles for borrowers
Limited to Florida only; not available in other states despite operating since 1989
Fee structure ($13 + $3 verification fee on $100 minimum) compounds quickly on repeated borrowing
Military active duty, spouses, and dependents are prohibited by federal law from obtaining cash advances
No credit-building benefit; loans do not report to credit bureaus to help improve credit scores
best_for: Floridians facing immediate cash shortfalls before payday with no other borrowing options
Consumers with bad credit or no credit history unable to qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
Borrowers needing $100-$1,000 with same-day or next-day access to physical cash in hand
Individuals who value convenience and 365-day/24-hour availability over loan cost minimization
diagnosis: Amscot is best for Florida residents facing genuine financial emergencies who need quick access to cash and lack other borrowing options. However, the 338.93% APR and rapid fee accumulation make payday loans a costly solution; borrowers should exhaust payday alternative loans (under 36% APR), credit union PALs, or emergency assistance programs before using this service.
services: Payday Loans (Amscot Cash Advances) up to $500
